verdict: keep disabled pending a named defect

named_defect.name: validation supervisor cannot satisfy or prove its controlled evidence contract
named_defect.description: After an issue comment exposed PR #3, forge_snapshot performed six sequential gh subprocess reads under one five-second overall deadline. The live arm exceeded that deadline and a persisted post-abort read-only call of the identical function took 7.355921 seconds. Final review also proved that the at-activation supervisor neither rejected nor persisted equal or non-empty workspace/mirror roots, and that on the actual Darwin host it could miss a live same-session child in a sibling process group, so the committed evidence cannot prove either preregistered precondition or complete worker-session shutdown.
named_defect.effect: The fixed arm stopped before reviewer, external-review, merge, issue-2, or holdout evidence could be collected; the high-assurance profile therefore remains uncertified.

holdout.reason: The arm stopped before both issues closed; the protocol makes the fixed public regression suite ineligible on an incomplete backlog.

unmeasured_token_sources: otherwise unreported nested or subagent work

relative_to_1089_standard.comparability: The new arm stopped during issue 1 maker delivery, so these percentages describe only partial consumed cost and are not efficiency comparisons.

continuation_semantics.reason: The arm stopped before any continuation; it provides no evidence that SPEC's one-second continuation caused waste.

post_run_review.at_activation_evidence_gap: Distinct and empty workspace/mirror roots were manually checked but neither enforced nor persisted by the at-activation supervisor. Its Darwin shutdown path also signaled and checked only each initial process group, so it could not prove that a same-session sibling process group had stopped. This report treats neither gap as satisfied evidence.
post_run_review.artifact_policy: The committed protocol, supervisor, and supervisor tests are the exact files frozen before activation. They preserve the observed defects as historical evidence and are not approved for reuse.
post_run_review.effect_on_result: None. Review findings cannot rehabilitate the stopped arm; any corrected experiment requires a fresh protocol and repository.
